title: saxifraga exarata subsp. ampullacea
intro: Saxifraga exarata subsp. ampullacea, a subspecies of the saxifrage genus, is a plant species that belongs to the Saxifragaceae family and is commonly known as Rock saxifrage or Rocky saxifrage. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Saxifraga exarata subsp. ampullacea include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. The herbal preparations of this plant often involve making tea from its leaves or roots, and it can also be used in infusions or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
